trait.name: Alseid Weapon Training
trait.text: You have proficiency with spears and shortbows.

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

trait.name: Light Hooves
trait.text: You have proficiency in the Stealth skill.

trait.name: Quadruped
trait.text: The mundane details of the structures of humanoids can present considerable obstacles for you. You have to squeeze when moving through trapdoors, manholes, and similar structures even when a Medium humanoid wouldn’t have to squeeze. In addition, ladders, stairs, and similar structures are difficult terrain for you.

trait.name: Type
trait.text: You are of the monstrosity type.

trait.name: Woodfriend
trait.text: When in a forest, you leave no tracks and can automatically discern true north.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Elvish and Trade Tongue.

trait.name: Bite
trait.text: Your powerful jaws are natural weapons, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If it hits, you deal 1d6 + your Strength modifier piercing damage.

trait.name: Natural Armor
trait.text: Your hide is thick and tough, granting you a natural AC of 13 + your Dexterity modifier.

trait.name: Powerful Build
trait.text: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

trait.name: Subrace
trait.text: Two main subraces of bearfolk exist: grizzlehides and purifiers. Choose one of them.

trait.name: Ursine Talent
trait.text: You have proficiency in the Athletics and Perception skills.

trait.name: Bear Hug
trait.text: When you take the Attack action, you can make an unarmed strike as a bonus action. If you hit, you can grapple the target in addition to dealing damage to it. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

trait.name: Thick Coat
trait.text: You are resistant to cold damage.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Common, and either Northern or Umbral.

trait.name: Bite
trait.text: Your powerful jaws are natural weapons, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If it hits, you deal 1d6 + your Strength modifier piercing damage.

trait.name: Natural Armor
trait.text: Your hide is thick and tough, granting you a natural AC of 13 + your Dexterity modifier.

trait.name: Powerful Build
trait.text: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

trait.name: Subrace
trait.text: Two main subraces of bearfolk exist: grizzlehides and purifiers. Choose one of them.

trait.name: Ursine Talent
trait.text: You have proficiency in the Athletics and Perception skills.

trait.name: Nature’s Gift
trait.text: You know one cantrip of your choice from the druid spell list. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability score for it.

trait.name: Stalwart Will
trait.text: When you make an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma saving throw, you can roll a d4 and add the result to your total. You can do this after the roll but before any effects occur. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Common, and either Northern or Umbral.

trait.name: Centaur Weapon Training
trait.text: You have proficiency with the pike and the longbow.

trait.name: Humanoid Torso
trait.text: Although you are Large, you wield weapons and wear armor sized for a Medium creature, thanks to the proportions of your humanoid torso.

trait.name: Natural Attacks
trait.text: You have proficiency with your hooves. You make one attack with your hooves, which deal 2d6 bludgeoning damage.

trait.name: Pike Charge
trait.text: If you move at least 30 feet straight toward a target and then hit it with a pike attack on the same turn, the target takes an extra 3 (1d6) piercing damage. The number of extra damage dice you gain increases by one at 6th level (2d6) and again at 11th level (3d6). You can apply this extra damage only once per turn. You can use this ability a number of times per day equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum 1), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

trait.name: Quadruped
trait.text: You have disadvantage on Stealth checks because of your size and indelicate hooves. The mundane details of the structures of humanoids can present considerable obstacles for you, such as ladders and manholes.

trait.name: Self-sufficient
trait.text: You have proficiency with the Medicine skill.

trait.name: Type
trait.text: You are of the monstrosity type.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Centaur and one other language of your choice.

trait.name: Eldritch Resilience
trait.text: You have advantage on Constitution saving throws against spells.

trait.name: Insanity
trait.text: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
trait.text: You have disadvantage on attack rolls and Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight when you, the target of your attack, or whatever you are trying to perceive is in direct sunlight.

trait.name: Superior Darkvision
trait.text: Accustomed to life underground, you can see in dim light within 120 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Dwarvish and your choice between Common and Undercommon.

trait.name: Dark Thirst
trait.text: You can bite as an unarmed strike against an incapacitated creature or one you have grappled. Your bite deals 1 point of piercing damage, and if the creature has blood and isn’t a construct or undead, you can feed from it as part of the attack. Your feeding deals necrotic damage equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1), and if your target takes damage you regain one spent hit die. You can regain a number of spent hit dice with this trait equal to your Constitution modifier per long rest (minimum of 1).

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: Thanks to your undead ancestry, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

trait.name: Predatory Charm
trait.text: As an action, you can magically beguile the mind of a creature that you can see within 30 feet. For 1 hour, you have advantage on Charisma checks made against the target. If you or any of your allies attack or damage the target, this effect ends. When the effect ends, the target feels repulsed by you and becomes hostile toward you until the next dawn, during which time it becomes immune to this effect. A hostile creature won’t necessarily attack outright, but it won’t deal with you in any way and might actively try to hinder you. You can use this trait once, and regain the ability to do so when you finish a short or long rest.
A creature immune to being charmed is immune to your predatory charm.

trait.name: Undead Resistance
trait.text: You have advantage on saving throws against disease, and you have resistance against necrotic damage.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Common.

trait.name: Commanding Presence
trait.text: You have proficiency in the Persuasion skill.

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

trait.name: Subrace
trait.text: There are five subraces of dragonkin, determined by the type of dragon they descend from: the Four Elemental Kinds and mixed heritage. Choose one of these subraces.

trait.name: Inner Flame
trait.text: You know the produce flame cantrip. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it.

trait.name: Resilient Scales
trait.text: You have resistance to fire damage.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Draconic and Trade Tongue.

trait.name: Commanding Presence
trait.text: You have proficiency in the Persuasion skill.

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

trait.name: Subrace
trait.text: There are five subraces of dragonkin, determined by the type of dragon they descend from: the Four Elemental Kinds and mixed heritage. Choose one of these subraces.

trait.name: Resilient Scales
trait.text: You have resistance to lightning damage.

trait.name: Storm Strike
trait.text: You know the shocking grasp cantrip. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Draconic and Trade Tongue.

trait.name: Commanding Presence
trait.text: You have proficiency in the Persuasion skill.

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

trait.name: Subrace
trait.text: There are five subraces of dragonkin, determined by the type of dragon they descend from: the Four Elemental Kinds and mixed heritage. Choose one of these subraces.

trait.name: Resilient Scales
trait.text: You have resistance to acid damage.

trait.name: Stone Resolve
trait.text: You know the blade ward cantrip. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Draconic and Trade Tongue.

trait.name: Commanding Presence
trait.text: You have proficiency in the Persuasion skill.

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

trait.name: Subrace
trait.text: There are five subraces of dragonkin, determined by the type of dragon they descend from: the Four Elemental Kinds and mixed heritage. Choose one of these subraces.

trait.name: Resilient Scales
trait.text: You have resistance to cold damage.

trait.name: Tide’s Grasp
trait.text: You know the poison spray cantrip. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Draconic and Trade Tongue.

trait.name: Commanding Presence
trait.text: You have proficiency in the Persuasion skill.

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

trait.name: Subrace
trait.text: There are five subraces of dragonkin, determined by the type of dragon they descend from: the Four Elemental Kinds and mixed heritage. Choose one of these subraces.

trait.name: Resilient Scales
trait.text: You have resistance to poison damage.

trait.name: Soldier’s Eye
trait.text: You know the shillelagh cantrip. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Draconic and Trade Tongue.

trait.name: Alien Mind
trait.text: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened.

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: Accustomed to life in the gloom, you can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

trait.name: Strange and Deadly Surroundings
trait.text: You have proficiency in the Stealth and Survival skills.

trait.name: Twisted
trait.text: When you attack a creature from hiding, the creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Dexterity modifier) or be frightened of you until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Goblin. The written form of the dust goblin dialect of Goblin is pictographic and capable of remarkable subtlety, though it is largely impenetrable to outsiders.

trait.name: Ability Score Increase
trait.text: Two different ability scores of your choice increase by 1.

trait.name: Constructed Body
trait.text: Your consciousness and soul reside within a soul gem to animate your mechanical body. As such, you are a living creature with some of the benefits and drawbacks of a construct.

* You cannot eat, drink, or breathe. You can’t drink potions or gain benefits that come from drinking, eating, or inhaling vapors. * You do not naturally sleep. * During a rest, you must perform maintenance on your gears, springs, and joints, following the normal rules governing rest and activity. While performing this maintenance, you are aware of your surroundings but you have disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks. If you go longer than 24 hours without performing maintenance (you don’t take a long rest), you gain one level of exhaustion. All exhaustion gained this way disappears after your next long rest. * You can’t be stabilized when dying with a Wisdom (Medicine) check or [[[srd-spell:spare the dying]]]. Instead, a successful DC 10 Intelligence check or a [[[srd-spell:mending]]] cantrip is needed. * You regain only one-half the usual number of hit points from spells or magical effects with the words cure, heal, or healing in their titles.

trait.name: Flesh of Steel
trait.text: You are immune to disease, poison damage, and the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Solid Construction
trait.text: If you are killed but your soul gem and memory gears are still intact, you can be restored to life if your body is repaired and soulforging is cast on it again. Because the body already exists, the cost of the ritual is just 500 gp, plus the cost of repairing the body (DM’s discretion, typically 1d4 x 50 gp). If your body was destroyed but your soul gem and memory tapes are intact, they can be implanted into a new body at the standard cost (10,000 gp). The only other magic capable of bringing you back from the dead is a wish spell, which restores you fully.

trait.name: Type
trait.text: You are of the humanoid (gearforged) type.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Machine Speech and Common.

trait.name: Bully
trait.text: Although gnolls are cowardly at heart, they like to disguise their fears by abusing others. You have disadvantage on saving throws against being frightened. On the other hand, whenever you make a Charisma (Intimidation) check for dealing with obviously smaller or weaker targets, you are considered proficient in the Intimidation skill and add double your proficiency bonus to the check, instead of your normal proficiency bonus.

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

trait.name: Gnoll Weapon Training
trait.text: You have proficiency with the spear, shortbow, longbow, light crossbow, and heavy crossbow.

trait.name: Live to Fight Another Day
trait.text: When you take the Disengage action, your base walking speed is increased by 10 feet.

trait.name: Scent
trait.text: You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Subraces
trait.text: Although not, strictly speaking, different races, gnolls from the civilized lands live in an environment so different than their savage brethren that they are considered separate subraces. Choose one of them.

trait.name: Obsequious
trait.text: Whenever you make a Charisma (Persuasion) check for dealing with obviously bigger or more powerful targets, you are considered proficient in the Persuasion skill and add double your proficiency bonus to the check, instead of your normal proficiency bonus.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Southern and Gnollish. Gnollish words sounds almost like growls for the uninitiated, and gnolls tend to use scents and small gestures to convey subtle meanings. Two gnolls speaking can almost seem like dogs barking at each other to a casual observer.

trait.name: Bully
trait.text: Although gnolls are cowardly at heart, they like to disguise their fears by abusing others. You have disadvantage on saving throws against being frightened. On the other hand, whenever you make a Charisma (Intimidation) check for dealing with obviously smaller or weaker targets, you are considered proficient in the Intimidation skill and add double your proficiency bonus to the check, instead of your normal proficiency bonus.

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

trait.name: Gnoll Weapon Training
trait.text: You have proficiency with the spear, shortbow, longbow, light crossbow, and heavy crossbow.

trait.name: Live to Fight Another Day
trait.text: When you take the Disengage action, your base walking speed is increased by 10 feet.

trait.name: Scent
trait.text: You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Subraces
trait.text: Although not, strictly speaking, different races, gnolls from the civilized lands live in an environment so different than their savage brethren that they are considered separate subraces. Choose one of them.

trait.name: Scavenge
trait.text: Whenever you make a Wisdom (Survival) check for gathering food or locating water, you are considered proficient in the Survival skill and add double your proficiency bonus to the check, instead of your normal proficiency bonus.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Southern and Gnollish. Gnollish words sounds almost like growls for the uninitiated, and gnolls tend to use scents and small gestures to convey subtle meanings. Two gnolls speaking can almost seem like dogs barking at each other to a casual observer.

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: Thanks to your jinn blood, you can see in dim light within 60 feet as though it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

trait.name: Desert Dependent
trait.text: For every month you remain away from the desert, you must make a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, you are afflicted with indefinite madness (see Madness; SRD). Roll on the table or work with your DM to determine the nature of your madness.

trait.name: Negotiator
trait.text: You have proficiency in the Persuasion skill.

trait.name: Siraati
trait.text: All jinnborn have an affinity for mystic paths, depending on the jinn patron of their tribe. Choose one of the following siraati: air, earth, fire, or water.

trait.name: Subrace
trait.text: Jinnborn are divided into subraces depending on the innate touch of the jinn. Choose one of the following subraces.

trait.name: Favor of the Jinn
trait.text: You can call upon your jinn patron to gain advantage on a saving throw or ability check, or to impose disadvantage on an attack roll against you. You must decide to use this ability before the relevant d20 roll. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

trait.name: Walker
trait.text: You have advantage on saving throws against being stunned and extreme environments, and on ability checks made to navigate the wilderness and avoid losing your way.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and one of the four elemental languages (Auran, Aquan, Ignan, or Terran). The elemental language must conform to your siraati.

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: Thanks to your jinn blood, you can see in dim light within 60 feet as though it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

trait.name: Desert Dependent
trait.text: For every month you remain away from the desert, you must make a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, you are afflicted with indefinite madness (see Madness; SRD). Roll on the table or work with your DM to determine the nature of your madness.

trait.name: Negotiator
trait.text: You have proficiency in the Persuasion skill.

trait.name: Siraati
trait.text: All jinnborn have an affinity for mystic paths, depending on the jinn patron of their tribe. Choose one of the following siraati: air, earth, fire, or water.

trait.name: Subrace
trait.text: Jinnborn are divided into subraces depending on the innate touch of the jinn. Choose one of the following subraces.

trait.name: Elemental Strike
trait.text: Once on your turn when you hit with a melee attack, you can deal an additional 1d6 damage. The damage type corresponds to your siraati (lightning for air, acid for earth, fire for fire, cold for water). You can use this ability a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1), and you regain all expended uses after finishing a long rest.

trait.name: Protection of the Jinn
trait.text: You have resistance against a type of damage that corresponds to your siraati (lightning for air, acid for earth, fire for fire, cold for water).

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and one of the four elemental languages (Auran, Aquan, Ignan, or Terran). The elemental language must conform to your siraati.

trait.name: Contained Fury
trait.text: When you’re badly hurt, your base urges send you into a fury. While below half your maximum hit points, you have access to the the following options: * Once per turn when you hit with a weapon attack, you deal an extra 2 (1d4) weapon damage. * As a reaction, when you are hit by an attack during your fury, you can add 2 to your AC against that attack. You must be able to see the attacker to take this reaction.

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as though it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

trait.name: Plantborn
trait.text: Though you enter a state of dormancy during long rests similar to the way other creatures sleep, you are immune to any effect that would put you to sleep. When you reach 7th level, you can cast the speak with plants spell as a 3rd-level spell once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

trait.name: Roots of the Past
trait.text: You have proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Arcana, History, Nature, or Religion.

trait.name: Type
trait.text: You are of the plant type.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Sylvan.

trait.name: Blindsider
trait.text: You have advantage on your attack roll against an enemy within 5 feet of you if you have an ally that’s not incapacitated also within 5 feet of the target. You can apply this bonus to one attack per round.

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
trait.text: You have disadvantage on attack rolls and on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight when you, the target of your attack, or whatever you are trying to perceive is in bright sunlight.

trait.name: Tinkerer
trait.text: You have proficiency with artisan’s tools of your choice: alchemist’s supplies, mason’s tools, smith’s tools, or tinker’s tools.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Draconic and Trade Tongue (Common).

trait.name: Dangerous Beauty
trait.text: You have proficiency in the Deception and Intimidation skills.

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: Thanks to your demonic heritage you can see dim light within 60 feet as though it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

trait.name: Serpent Strike
trait.text: You have advantage on attack rolls against a creature you have surprised, or that is charmed by you or your allies.

trait.name: Snake Body
trait.text: You have advantage on saving throws and ability checks against being knocked prone. You can’t benefit from anything that requires legs or feet (such as magical footwear).

trait.name: Type
trait.text: You are of the monstrosity type.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Abyssal and Common.

trait.name: Charge
trait.text: If you move at least 10 feet toward a target and hit it with a horn attack in the same turn, you deal an extra 1d6 piercing damage and you can shove the target 5 feet as a bonus action. You can apply this extra damage once per turn. At 11th level, when you shove a creature with Charge, you can push it 10 feet instead of 5. You can use this ability a number of times per day equal to your Constitution modifier, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

trait.name: Labyrinth Sense
trait.text: You can retrace without error any path you have previously taken, with no ability check.

trait.name: Natural Attacks
trait.text: Your horns are natural weapons, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with them, they deal 1d6 + your Strength modifier piercing damage.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Minotaur, as well as one other language of your choice (typically the Trade Tongue or Southern languages).

trait.name: Arcane Heritage
trait.text: You can ignore class requirements when attuning to a magic item.

trait.name: Mystical Understanding
trait.text: You have proficiency in the Arcana skill.

trait.name: Spell Damping
trait.text: You have advantage on Strength and Dexterity saving throws against spells.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Giant.

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: Accustomed to living underground and in other dingy places, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. To a distance of 60 feet from yourself, you can see in dim light as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

trait.name: Nimbleness
trait.text: You can move through a hostile creature’s space in combat as long as it is size Medium or larger.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
trait.text: You have advantage on your attack roll against a creature if at least one of your allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

trait.name: Rodent Empathy
trait.text: You have advantage on Handle Animal checks to influence the behavior of rodents.

trait.name: Swim
trait.text: Ratfolk are capable, if slow, swimmers. You have a swimming speed of 10 feet.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Common.

trait.name: Mimicry
trait.text: Ravenfolk can mimic any sound they’ve heard. Make a Charisma (Deception) check against the passive Wisdom (Insight) of any listeners. Success indicates they believe the sound you created was real.

trait.name: Sudden Attack
trait.text: You have advantage on attack rolls against a surprised creature.

trait.name: Trickster
trait.text: You have proficiency in the Deception and Stealth skills.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Huginn’s Speech and Northern Tongue.

trait.name: Blood Frenzy
trait.text: As a bonus action, you can enter a blood frenzy until the end of your turn. While in blood frenzy you have advantage on melee attack rolls against any creature that doesn’t have all its hit points. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

trait.name: Limited Amphibiousness
trait.text: You can breathe air and water, but you need to be submerged at least once every 4 hours or you begin suffocating.

trait.name: Natural Armor
trait.text: Your thick hide grants you an AC of 12 + your Dexterity modifier.

trait.name: Natural Attacks
trait.text: You have proficiency with your claws and bite. Your claws deal 1d4 slashing damage, and your bite deals 1d4 piercing damage.

trait.name: Shark Telepathy
trait.text: You can magically command one shark within 120 feet of you using limited telepathy. This command is limited to simple concepts such as “come here,” “defend me,” or “attack this target.”

trait.name: Superior Darkvision
trait.text: Accustomed to living in the dark depths of the ocean, you can see in dim light within 120 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Aquan and Common.

trait.name: Ability Score Increase
trait.text: One of your physical ability scores (Strength, Dexterity, Constitution) increases by 2, and one of your mental ability scores (Intelligence, Wisdom, Charisma) increases by 2. You also take a –2 penalty to any one ability score. You may apply this penalty to the same ability score to which you gave a +2 bonus, granting you an overall +2 bonus to any one ability score.

trait.name: Gliding Wings
trait.text: You take no damage from falls. You gain a fly speed of 40 feet but cannot hover. At the end of any round you fly, you must have descended at least one-quarter the distance you travelled or you fall.

trait.name: Natural Armor
trait.text: Your Armor Class cannot be less than 11 + your Dexterity modifier no matter what armor you are wearing.

trait.name: Natural Attacks
trait.text: You have proficiency with your claws, which deal 1d4 slashing damage.

trait.name: Stalker
trait.text: You have proficiency in the Perception skill and Stealth skill.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Tosculi and one other language of your choice (typically Southern).

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

trait.name: Inhuman Vigor
trait.text: You concentrate regenerative power in your blood to swiftly recover from wounds. As a bonus action, you can expend one hit die to regain hit points as if you finished a short rest. The number of hit dice you can expend increases by one when you reach 6th level (2 hit dice), 12th level (3 hit dice), and 18th level (4 hit dice). You must complete a long rest before using this ability again. If you take acid or fire damage, you lose access to this ability until you finish a short or long rest.

trait.name: Legacy of Fear
trait.text: You have proficiency in the Intimidation skill.

trait.name: Natural Weapons
trait.text: You have large fangs and sharp claws instead of fingernails and toenails. You can use your claws or fangs to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with them, claws deal 1d4 + Strength modifier slashing damage, and fangs deal 1d4 + Strength modifier piercing damage.

trait.name: Subrace
trait.text: Trollkin bear different traits depending on the type of creatures in their lineage. The two most common types of trollkin are night whispers and stonehides. Choose one of these subraces.

trait.name: Spirit Whispers
trait.text: The spirits of your tribe’s ancestors whisper secrets from beyond the veil of death. Before making an ability check or saving throw, you can heed the wisdom of the spirits to gain advantage on the roll. You cannot use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Northern Tongue.

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

trait.name: Inhuman Vigor
trait.text: You concentrate regenerative power in your blood to swiftly recover from wounds. As a bonus action, you can expend one hit die to regain hit points as if you finished a short rest. The number of hit dice you can expend increases by one when you reach 6th level (2 hit dice), 12th level (3 hit dice), and 18th level (4 hit dice). You must complete a long rest before using this ability again. If you take acid or fire damage, you lose access to this ability until you finish a short or long rest.

trait.name: Legacy of Fear
trait.text: You have proficiency in the Intimidation skill.

trait.name: Natural Weapons
trait.text: You have large fangs and sharp claws instead of fingernails and toenails. You can use your claws or fangs to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with them, claws deal 1d4 + Strength modifier slashing damage, and fangs deal 1d4 + Strength modifier piercing damage.

trait.name: Subrace
trait.text: Trollkin bear different traits depending on the type of creatures in their lineage. The two most common types of trollkin are night whispers and stonehides. Choose one of these subraces.

trait.name: Thick Hide
trait.text: Your skin is knobby, thick, and tough, granting you a +1 bonus to Armor Class.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Northern Tongue.

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

trait.name: Hybrid Form
trait.text: In your hybrid leonine form you grow sharp teeth and claws, and take on a more intimidating feline appearance. In this hybrid form you may use weapons, wear armor, and carry equipment normally. Your base walking speed increases to 40 feet. You have proficiency with your claws and bite. Your claws deal 1d4 slashing damage, and are considered light weapons. Your bite deals 1d4 piercing damage. In addition, you gain advantage on Charisma (Intimidate) checks, and Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell. You gain disadvantage on Intelligence checks and all other Charisma checks.

trait.name: Lion Form
trait.text: You may assume the form of a young lion (use the stat block for the panther) as if using the druid’s Wild Shape class feature. Unlike that feature, you retain your own hit points between forms. At 8th level you instead assume the form of a full-grown lion when you use this ability.

trait.name: Natural Shapechanger
trait.text: Natural werelions possess the magical ability to assume two alternate forms—the hybrid leonine and the lion. As an action, you can change into either your hybrid leonine form or your lion form. You can remain in this form for 1 hour, reverting to your humanoid form when the time expires, you fall unconscious, you drop to 0 hit points, or you die. You can revert to your humanoid form early as a bonus action. Once you use this ability, you can’t use it again until you finish a short rest.

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Southern and Sylvan.

trait.name: Shadow Fey Weapon Training
trait.text: You have proficiency with the rapier, shortsword, shortbow, and longbow

trait.name: Path of Shadows
trait.text: When in darkness, dim light, or a shadow large enough to cover your body, you can cast the misty step spell. You can use this ability a number of times per day equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
trait.text: You have disadvantage on attack rolls and on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight when you, the target of your attack, or whatever you are trying to perceive is in bright sunlight.

trait.name: Traveler in Darkness
trait.text: You have advantage on any Intelligence (Arcana) check to learn about a particular shadow road and how it functions.

trait.name: Extra Language
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Umbral.

trait.name: Ability Score Increase
trait.text: Two different ability scores of your choice increase by 1.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 39

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: Thanks to your elf blood, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 39

trait.name: Fey Ancestry
trait.text: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put you to sleep.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 39

trait.name: Skill Versatility
trait.text: You gain proficiency in two skills of your choice.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 39

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Common, Elvish, and one extra language of your choice.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 39

trait.name: Warriors, Dancers, Lovers
trait.text: Kariv humans are strong, lithe, and passionate. Raise your Strength, Dexterity, and Charisma scores by 1 each. You have proficiency in Performance and Survival
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 31

trait.name: Cursed
trait.text: You have advantage on Charisma checks if your last two long rests were taken at least a mile apart. Otherwise, you have disadvantage on Charisma checks
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 31

trait.name: Skills
trait.text: You gain proficiency in one skill of your choice.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 31

trait.name: Feat
trait.text: You gain one feat of your choice.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 31

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and one extra language of your choice. Humans typically learn the languages of other peoples they deal with, including obscure dialects. They are fond of sprinkling their speech with words borrowed from other tongues: Orc curses, Elvish musical expressions, Dwarvish military phrases, and so on.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 31

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: Accustomed to life underground, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 20

trait.name: Dwarven Resilience
trait.text: You have advantage on saving throws against poison, and you have resistance against poison damage.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 20

trait.name: Dwarven Combat Training
trait.text: You have proficiency with the battleaxe, handaxe, light hammer, and warhammer.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 20

trait.name: Tool Proficiency
trait.text: You gain proficiency with the artisan's tools of your choice: smith's tools, brewer's supplies, or mason's tools.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 20

trait.name: Stonecunning
trait.text: Whenever you make an Intelligence (History) check related to the origin of stonework, you are considered proficient in the History skill and add double your proficiency bonus to the check, instead of your normal proficiency bonus.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 20

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Dwarvish. Dwarvish is full of hard consonants and guttural sounds, and those characteristics spill over into whatever other language a dwarf might speak.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 20

trait.name: Dwarven Armor Training
trait.text: You have proficiency with light and medium armor.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 20

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: Accustomed to life underground, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 20

trait.name: Dwarven Resilience
trait.text: You have advantage on saving throws against poison, and you have resistance against poison damage.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 20

trait.name: Dwarven Combat Training
trait.text: You have proficiency with the battleaxe, handaxe, light hammer, and warhammer.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 20

trait.name: Tool Proficiency
trait.text: You gain proficiency with the artisan's tools of your choice: smith's tools, brewer's supplies, or mason's tools.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 20

trait.name: Stonecunning
trait.text: Whenever you make an Intelligence (History) check related to the origin of stonework, you are considered proficient in the History skill and add double your proficiency bonus to the check, instead of your normal proficiency bonus.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 20

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Dwarvish. Dwarvish is full of hard consonants and guttural sounds, and those characteristics spill over into whatever other language a dwarf might speak.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 20

trait.name: Dwarven Toughness
trait.text: Your hit point maximum increases by 1, and it increases by 1 every time you gain a level.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 20

trait.name: Ability Score Increase
trait.text: Your Intelligence score increases by 1.
Source: Midgard

trait.name: Dwarven Mysticism
trait.text: You gain proficiency with alchemist’s supplies, and you know either the guidance or resistance cantrip.
Source: Midgard

trait.name: Darkvision
trait.text: Accustomed to life underground, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 20

trait.name: Dwarven Resilience
trait.text: You have advantage on saving throws against poison, and you have resistance against poison damage.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 20

trait.name: Dwarven Combat Training
trait.text: You have proficiency with the battleaxe, handaxe, light hammer, and warhammer.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 20

trait.name: Tool Proficiency
trait.text: You gain proficiency with the artisan's tools of your choice: smith's tools, brewer's supplies, or mason's tools.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 20

trait.name: Stonecunning
trait.text: Whenever you make an Intelligence (History) check related to the origin of stonework, you are considered proficient in the History skill and add double your proficiency bonus to the check, instead of your normal proficiency bonus.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 20

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Dwarvish. Dwarvish is full of hard consonants and guttural sounds, and those characteristics spill over into whatever other language a dwarf might speak.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 20

trait.name: Dwarven Toughness
trait.text: Your hit point maximum increases by 1, and it increases by 1 every time you gain a level.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 20

trait.name: Lucky
trait.text: When you roll a 1 on an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 28

trait.name: Brave
trait.text: You have advantage on saving throws against being frightened.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 28

trait.name: Halfling Nimbleness
trait.text: You can move through the space of any creature that is of a size larger than yours.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 28

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Halfling. The Halfling language isn't secret, but halflings are loath to share it with others. They write very little, so they don't have a rich body of literature. Their oral tradition, however, is very strong. Almost all halflings speak Common to converse with the people in whose lands they dwell or through which they are traveling.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 28

trait.name: Naturally Stealthy
trait.text: You can attempt to hide even when you are obscured only by a creature that is at least one size larger than you.
Source: Player's Handbook, p. 28

trait.name: Hardened
trait.text: You have spent your life surviving in the cold and wild places of Domovogrod. Your Constitution score increases by 1, and you recover from all levels of exhaustion at the end of a long rest.
Source: Midgard

trait.name: Old Souls
trait.text: You have a connection to the elder powers of Midgard, and they recognize your merits. You can use your Wisdom modifier instead of Intelligence when making History and Religion checks.
Source: Midgard
